tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zaznacz, ktore zdania (1.1-1.4) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information). Nastepnie odpowiedz pelnym zdaniem na pytanie 1.5.
tasks.text:
THE MODERN OLYMPIC GAMES

The modern Olympic Games owe their existence to a French educator named Baron Pierre de Coubertin. Inspired by the ancient Greek athletic competitions held at Olympia, Coubertin believed that international sporting events could promote peace and understanding between nations. After years of campaigning, he succeeded in organising the first modern Olympics, which took place in Athens, Greece, in 1896.

The 1896 Games were modest by today's standards. Only 241 athletes from 14 countries took part, and all of them were men. Women were first allowed to compete in the 1900 Paris Olympics, though only in a limited number of events such as tennis, sailing, and golf. It was not until the 2012 London Olympics that every participating country included at least one female athlete in its team.

The Olympic motto, 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' — meaning 'Faster, Higher, Stronger' — was adopted in 1894 and has guided the spirit of the Games ever since. In 2021, the International Olympic Committee added the word 'Communiter', meaning 'Together', to reflect the importance of unity and solidarity in modern sport.

Hosting the Olympics is both a privilege and a challenge. Cities spend billions of dollars building stadiums, transport links, and accommodation for athletes. While the Games can boost tourism and international prestige, many host cities have struggled with the financial burden long after the closing ceremony. Athens, for example, was left with numerous empty venues that fell into disrepair. In response to such concerns, the IOC has encouraged recent host cities to use existing facilities or build temporary structures that can be removed after the Games.

Despite these challenges, the Olympic Games remain the world's largest sporting event, bringing together over 10,000 athletes from more than 200 countries every four years.

items.statement: Coubertin's main motivation for reviving the Olympics was to encourage physical fitness among young Europeans.
items.explanation: FALSE — The text says Coubertin 'believed that international sporting events could promote peace and understanding between nations', not physical fitness.

items.statement: Women had to wait over a century from the first modern Olympics before achieving full representation.
items.explanation: TRUE — The first modern Olympics were in 1896, and it was not until the 2012 London Olympics (116 years later) that 'every participating country included at least one female athlete'.

items.statement: Athens made a profit from hosting the 1896 Olympic Games.
items.explanation: NO INFORMATION — The text mentions Athens was left with empty venues after hosting (presumably a later Games), but says nothing about the financial outcome of the 1896 Games.

items.statement: The IOC has responded to the financial problems of host cities by changing its approach to venue construction.
items.explanation: TRUE — The text states 'the IOC has encouraged recent host cities to use existing facilities or build temporary structures that can be removed after the Games'.

openQuestion.question: Why was the word 'Communiter' added to the Olympic motto in 2021?
openQuestion.modelAnswer: It was added to reflect the importance of unity and solidarity / togetherness in modern sport.

vocabItems.definition: to support or help something grow, develop, or become more widespread
not large or impressive in size or scale
the high status, respect, and admiration that comes from success or achievement
a state of being damaged or in bad condition because of lack of care
unity and mutual support within a group, especially in difficult times
